multiplechoiceresponse.label:
A clinician asks why the decision tree predicts Disease for a particular patient.You examine the tree and see that the patient reaches a leaf node where:
most training patients had disease
the path to the leaf includes specific conditions on thal_2, age, and ca_1 Which explanation best reflects how the decision tree justifies its prediction?

choicegroup.choice: “The model predicts Disease because thal_2, age, and ca_1 are among the most important features in the tree overall.”
“The model predicts Disease because the patient satisfies a sequence of conditions that led many similar training patients to have disease.”
“The model predicts Disease because the combined effect of these features increases predicted risk in an additive way.”
“The model predicts Disease because each of these features individually points toward higher risk.”

div.p: A decision tree explains predictions by routing a patient through a sequence of conditional rules and comparing them to training cases that followed the same path. The prediction reflects how those similar cases were classified, not a global ranking of feature importance, an additive combination of effects, or the individual influence of each feature in isolation. This rule-based, path-specific reasoning is what makes decision trees particularly interpretable in practice.
